Center: US National Nuclear Data Center, Brookhaven, USA
ref: Jour: Physical Review Letters, Vol.130, p.232301 (2023)
title: beta+ Gamow-Teller Strengths from Unstable 14O via the (d,2He) Reaction in Inverse Kinematics

x4freetext: beta+ Gamow-Teller Strengths from Unstable 14O via
the (d,2He) Reaction in Inverse Kinematics

x4codes.shortRef: Jour: Physical Review Letters, Vol.130, p.232301 (2023)

x4freetext: #doi:10.1103/PhysRevLett.130.232301

x4codes.hlp: Michigan State University, East Lansing, MI, United States of America
Universidad de Santiago de Compostela, Spain

x4codes.hlp: Michigan State University, East Lansing, MI, United States of America

x4freetext: The Coupled Cyclotron Facility at the
National Superconducting Cyclotron Laboratory (NSCL).
A 10- to 50-pnA, 150-MeV/nucleon beam of 16O was
accelerated and struck a 1316-mg/cm2-thick Be
production target. A 150-mg/cm2-thick Al degrader was
used in the A1900 fragment separator to produce a
70%-pure 14O beam at 105 MeV/nucleon and with
intensities between 0.2 and 0.7 Mpps. The time-of-
flight (TOF) between two scintillators placed at the
exit of the A1900 and the entrance of the S800
spectrograph beam line (the S800 object point) was
used to separate 14O from 13N (23%) and 12C (7%)
contaminants on an event-by-event basis.
The ATTPC used for the first time
with a fast rare-isotope beam, was filled with pure
deuterium gas. The gas of the active volume was
isolated from the beam line and S800 vacuum by
12-microm-thick polyamide windows. The fragment
identification was performed event by event using the
TOF between scintillators at the S800 object point
and at the focal plane of S800, and the energy loss
of the fragments in an ionization chamber at the
focal plane of S800.
Pure deuterium gas at a pressure of 530 Torr (+-0.5%),
corresponding to a thickness of 11.7 mg/cm2.
The excitation energy of 14N produced after the
14O(d,2He) reaction is reconstructed in three steps.
First, the electron cloud produced by ionizing
charged particles in the ATTPC is analyzed with
pattern recognition and fitting routines to identify
the (d,2He) events and extract the 2He momentum. The
selection of (d,2He) events is ensured by the
coincidence between an identified 14O ion in the beam,
a relevant residual fragment in the S800, and two
fitted tracks with a minimal distance smaller than
2sigma of the minimal- distance distribution (<1 cm)
in the beam region. The point of minimal distance
defines the reaction vertex. The energy (deduced from
the range) and angular resolutions of a single track
are about 15 keV and 1.5 degree, respectively. The
momentum vectors of the two protons are used to
reconstruct the 2He momentum vector and, through an
invariant-mass calculation, its internal energy.
Finally, the excitation energy of 14N is obtained
from a missing-mass calculation.

x4code.headerHelp: Error in value of quantity, defined under ERR-ANALYS

x4freetext: The error bars in the data include
statistical and systematic uncertainties. The latter
are dominated by uncertainties in the acceptance
corrections and beam intensity determination, but are
relatively small compared to the statistical
uncertainties, except for the state at 3.95 MeV, for
which both types of uncertainty are comparable.
Approved by R.G.T.Zegers on July 30, 2025.
BP
